retrieved.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
retrieved.text:
Almost all bachelor’s, teacher accreditation and diploma programmes require you to complete an introductory and orientation period (STEOP) during the first semester.

The aim of the STEOP is to give you an insight into your studies and enable you to assess

- whether the course contents are in line with your personal expectations in the programme, and
- if you can meet the requirements for the degree programme.

Only after passing all STEOP examinations, you can attend additional courses and take further examinations. All examination dates are scheduled in such a way that you can continue your studies in the subsequent semester, as soon as you have successfully completed the STEOP.

You can retake examinations three times. If the fourth examination attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the examination. A resumption of this degree programme is not possible at the University of Vienna. You are also permanently excluded from all degree programmes in which this negatively assessed examimination is compulsory according to the curriculum.

You can find information about your degree programme’s STEOP and the relevant dates in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

retrieved.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
retrieved.text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.

If you do not take a certain number of exams, it does not have any consequences for you personally in terms of study law. Students can have various reasons why they temporarily take fewer exams: gainful employment, family duties and care obligations. - If you are not able to take exams for a certain time due to personal reasons, you can apply for a [leave of absence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/leave-of-absence/). - Also students enrolled in more than one degree programme often take fewer exams. retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
heading_path: What do I need a confirmation of withdrawal for?
retrieved.text:
- It is a proof that you are no longer admitted to the respective degree programme at the University of Vienna.
- Different authorities in Austria and abroad may demand a confirmation of withdrawal.
- If you want to be admitted to the same degree programme at another Austrian university you have to present your confirmation of withdrawal there.

A confirmation of withdrawal is also available if you close only one of multiple degree programmes.

You do not receive a confirmation of withdrawal if you successfully complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna or complete all supplementary examinations at the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities.

retrieved.title: Citing and labelling AI
retrieved.text:
In order to ensure the visibility of your independent contribution, it is essential to present your work transparently and in a manner that allows for clear and accurate tracking of the use of AI. It is crucial to clearly distinguish the parts of the work that originate from you, ensuring that your independent performance is accurately assessed. Failure to disclose the use of AI could be considered a breach of the principles of good academic practice.

The responsibility for determining how the use of AI is to be made transparent lies with the respective lecturer, the study programme director (SPL) or the respective department. In the absence of specific requirements, established best practices may serve as a useful guideline.

A distinction is made between references within the text and entries in the bibliography and, secondly, between content generated entirely by AI and content produced with the assistance of AI.

reranked.text:
Almost all bachelor’s, teacher accreditation and diploma programmes require you to complete an introductory and orientation period (STEOP) during the first semester.

The aim of the STEOP is to give you an insight into your studies and enable you to assess

- whether the course contents are in line with your personal expectations in the programme, and
- if you can meet the requirements for the degree programme.

Only after passing all STEOP examinations, you can attend additional courses and take further examinations. All examination dates are scheduled in such a way that you can continue your studies in the subsequent semester, as soon as you have successfully completed the STEOP.

You can retake examinations three times. If the fourth examination attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the examination. A resumption of this degree programme is not possible at the University of Vienna. You are also permanently excluded from all degree programmes in which this negatively assessed examimination is compulsory according to the curriculum.

You can find information about your degree programme’s STEOP and the relevant dates in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

reranked.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
reranked.text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.

reranked.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
reranked.text:
STEOP-Mentoring aims to support you during your first semester at the University of Vienna! Small peer-goups led by student STEOP-mentors serve as a voluntary addition to the lectures of the introductory and orientation period (STEOP) and are there to help you in mastering its challenges.

[You find further information (available in German) here.](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/services-zur-qualitaet-von-studien/steop-mentoring/informationen-fuer-studierende/)

reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
heading_path: What do I need a confirmation of withdrawal for?
reranked.text:
- It is a proof that you are no longer admitted to the respective degree programme at the University of Vienna.
- Different authorities in Austria and abroad may demand a confirmation of withdrawal.
- If you want to be admitted to the same degree programme at another Austrian university you have to present your confirmation of withdrawal there.

A confirmation of withdrawal is also available if you close only one of multiple degree programmes.

You do not receive a confirmation of withdrawal if you successfully complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna or complete all supplementary examinations at the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities.

reranked.title: Citing and labelling AI
reranked.text:
In order to ensure the visibility of your independent contribution, it is essential to present your work transparently and in a manner that allows for clear and accurate tracking of the use of AI. It is crucial to clearly distinguish the parts of the work that originate from you, ensuring that your independent performance is accurately assessed. Failure to disclose the use of AI could be considered a breach of the principles of good academic practice.

The responsibility for determining how the use of AI is to be made transparent lies with the respective lecturer, the study programme director (SPL) or the respective department. In the absence of specific requirements, established best practices may serve as a useful guideline.

A distinction is made between references within the text and entries in the bibliography and, secondly, between content generated entirely by AI and content produced with the assistance of AI.

selected.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
selected.text:
Almost all bachelor’s, teacher accreditation and diploma programmes require you to complete an introductory and orientation period (STEOP) during the first semester.

The aim of the STEOP is to give you an insight into your studies and enable you to assess

- whether the course contents are in line with your personal expectations in the programme, and
- if you can meet the requirements for the degree programme.

Only after passing all STEOP examinations, you can attend additional courses and take further examinations. All examination dates are scheduled in such a way that you can continue your studies in the subsequent semester, as soon as you have successfully completed the STEOP.

You can retake examinations three times. If the fourth examination attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the examination. A resumption of this degree programme is not possible at the University of Vienna. You are also permanently excluded from all degree programmes in which this negatively assessed examimination is compulsory according to the curriculum.

You can find information about your degree programme’s STEOP and the relevant dates in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

selected.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
selected.text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.
